column13: Two specialized guides. Repeat in 42 days — fastest repeat in sample. Strong Gate A signal if conversion rate holds. Acquisition via Kit email.

Survey response: gift buyer. But product choice (regional edibles) and source (Pinterest organic pin) suggests forager. Mixed signal. LTV estimate midpoint.
Homesteader cohort confirmed. Email acquisition — welcome sequence click-through. Awaiting repeat data. LTV estimate based on homesteader 2–4x/year pattern.

column2: Etsy receipt_id or internal order reference (ORD-YYYY-NNN format).
Date the order was created (YYYY-MM-DD). Source: Etsy API created_timestamp converted from UTC unix.
Full title of the guide or bundle purchased. For multi-item orders record the primary guide by revenue weight.
Category of the primary guide: wild_edibles | medicinal_plants | native_plants | complete_bundle | survival_prep | mushroom_id. Matches guide_type dimension in etsy-ga4-event-tracking.md.
Customer cohort assignment. One of: high_intent_forager | survival_prepper | homesteader | gift_buyer. See customer-cohort-analysis-framework.md Section 1–4 for full definitions and signal logic.
Date of this customer's first-ever Seedwarden purchase (YYYY-MM-DD). If this is the first purchase it equals the date column. If this is a repeat order it is earlier than the date column.

Number of additional purchases this customer has made after their first order. 0 = first-time buyer with no repeat yet. 1 = one repeat purchase. Increment each time a repeat purchase is logged in customer-analytics.csv.

For repeat buyers: number of days between first_purchase_date and the most recent repeat purchase date. Leave blank for customers still in their first 90-day window. Target ranges: high_intent_forager 30–60 days; homesteader 60–90 days; survival_prepper 60–120 days; gift_buyer 300–365 days.

Lifetime value estimate in USD. Calculation: (avg_order_value) x (estimated_annual_purchases) x (estimated_years_retained). For first-time buyers with no repeat data use cohort benchmark (forager: $180; prepper: $220; homesteader: $160; gift: $45). Update as actual repeat data accumulates. Net of Etsy 6.5% transaction fee and $0.20 listing fee per item.
Two-letter US state or CA province code. Source: Etsy API state field. Used for regional guide demand analysis and cannibalization detection.

Traffic source that brought this buyer to Etsy. Derived from GA4 session data (cross-reference purchase date with GA4 traffic acquisition by date). One of: etsy_organic | kit_email | pinterest | instagram | reddit | direct | unknown.

Flag for Phase 2 gate relevance. One of: Gate_A_watch (high conversion rate signal) | Gate_B_watch (high repeat rate signal) | Gate_C_watch (endangered species demand) | Gate_D_watch (paid ads readiness) | none. Update as 90-day window closes.
Qualitative observations. Record any unusual purchase patterns or data quality issues.

YYYY-MM — the calendar month of first purchase.

spring_planning (Jan-Apr) | long_tail_spring (May-Jun) | preservation (Jul-Sep) | long_tail_fall (Oct) | holiday_gift (Nov-Dec). Maps to seasonal demand calendar in customer-cohort-analysis-framework.md.
Count of unique buyer_user_ids who placed their first-ever Seedwarden order in this month. Source: Etsy API receipts filtered to first-purchase buyers (buyer_user_id not seen in prior months).
Always 100.0 — baseline month.
Count of buyers from this cohort who placed any order in the second calendar month of their lifecycle.
month_2_buyers / buyers_acquired * 100. Target: 8%+ for non-gift cohorts.

month_3_buyers / buyers_acquired * 100. Target: 15%+ (equivalent to 90-day second-purchase rate). This is the primary Phase 2 decision metric. See post-launch-analytics-framework.md Section 6, Gate B.
month_6_buyers / buyers_acquired * 100. Target: 10%+ for non-gift cohorts. Gift buyer target: 3%+.
Average cumulative net revenue per buyer in this cohort through Month 1. Net = gross * 0.905 - $0.25.
Average cumulative net revenue per buyer through Month 6. Primary LTV figure for Phase 2 gate decisions.
Percentage who made a second purchase within 90 days of first purchase. Target: 20%+. Primary health indicator.
Mean days between first and second purchase for repeat buyers. Target: 45-75 days (forager); 60-90 days (homesteader); 60-120 days (prepper).
Percentage of second purchases from a different guide category than first purchase. High rate = healthy catalog breadth.
